greet.text: Jammer polishes a glass that's already clean, watching the door with the flat calm of a man who's been besieged before. "Kid, in my day we called this a *box job*. Somebody wants everyone in this club to stay put while something happens somewhere else." He sets the glass down. "You want the tactical picture or a drink? Drink's faster."

why.text: "Nightclub, nothing. They want the people *in* it — the hoodoo men, the girl if she surfaces, and maybe one skinny cowboy with a famous new handle." His eyes measure you, not unkindly. "Word's moved, Count. You're inventory now. Get used to it."

brief.text: "Street's filling since noon — Gothicks mostly, some Kasuals, gangs that hate each other holding hands. That means outside money and a lot of it." He ticks points on scarred fingers. "Power's cut to the block, freight elevator's dead, and there's a jamming van on the corner dressed like a taco truck. Phones are dirt. Only clean line out of this club—" he nods toward the office "—is my deck. Old, but it's got teeth."

deck.text: "Somebody does." He looks at you a long moment, then reaches under the bar and tosses Jackie the office keys. "I flew that rig when your mother was in school, kid. Hip's gone; hands ain't. But this run wants young reflexes and old luck, and Jackie says the loa like you." A grim smile. "Try to be likable."

over.text: Jammer stands at the slats a long time, watching leather and hairspray drain out of the plaza like water finding a broken main. "No paymaster, no siege," he says softly. "Somebody reached out and unplugged the money." He turns, and gives you the nod cowboys give each other in his stories — the real ones. "Whatever you did in there, Count — that was a *run*. First drink's free for the rest of your life. Second one, I know you're good for."

bye.text: "Office is east side," Jammer says, back to watching his door. "And kid — Jackie goes in with you, but the ice out there tonight is *hungry*. You hear me? You jack out the second it goes wrong. Heroes make lousy regulars."
